title: "no splash screen-very very urgent.please"
date: 2007-08-15

### Post by surenkris on 2007-08-15
Hello gurus
i am using ubuntu 6.10 and recently i ran kleansweep and it happened to delete some of the useful files of ubuntu. when i reboot the machine there is no splash screen  for login and black screen appears showing 
Starting up......
Ubuntu 6.10 (none) tty1
(none) login:

when i enter the login name or whatever, it is still coming to the same message
Ubuntu 6.10 (none) tty1
(none) login:

Please help me how to login to the machine and recover the deleted files?
i have important data of about 30 GB and i cannot format it.i hope there is a solution to this.
thanks
Suren

---

### Post by shad0w_walker on 2007-08-15
Do you still have the Ubuntu installer disc about? 6.10 should be able to let you boot from the CD into a full version of Ubuntu (Will be slow but its a start) You should be able to access you files from there with a bit of luck.

---

### Post by surenkris on 2007-08-15
i do have the installation disk 6.10 and i tried to run ubuntu from cd, and after i did i could see my hard disk sda1 but i could not access the files bcas it is showing only the raw hard disk.

---

### Post by shad0w_walker on 2007-08-15
When you are using the cd, open up a terminal (Applications > Accessories > Terminal) and use these commands:

```
 
sudo -s
mkdir /media/sda1
mount /dev/sda1 /media/sda1
gksu nautilus /media/sda1

```

This will create a folder to mount your drive, mount it and then open that folder in nautilus. Nautilus will be opened with root privileges so you won't have any access denied error messages.

---

### Post by surenkris on 2007-08-15
Hello shadow
I followed ur instructions and i am able to see my files in sda1.
so how to recover the lost files by kleansweep?
i have the archive file which is created by kleansweep before it started the cleaning process.
the archive file is kleansweep-20070814-204052.tar.gz.
so how can this help me to recover or restore the lost files?
thanks once again.
suren

---

### Post by shad0w_walker on 2007-08-15
I haven't personally encountered kleansweep so i don't know its exact workings, however i guess that the file would be a mirror of the directorys/files that it cleaned up. You MAY be able to just extract it to the root of your hard drive (/media/sda1) however you should probably have a look around for any better information on restoring these files.

### Post by shad0w_walker on 2007-08-15

### Post by frafu on 2007-08-17
The Accessibility Forum being related to Assistive Technology, I have moved this thread to the General Help Forum where it might get more attention. 

Francesco

### Post by bigboy_pdb on 2007-10-12
surenkris, I know it has been a while since you last posted so I'm not certain if you still need help, but I think that it might be a good idea to back up the files elsewhere (such as an external hard drive) and then to re-install Ubuntu. If you need help doing this then please post back and I can assist you in backing up your files to an external hard drive from the command line.

---
